metadata.title: Six Slots, One Better Brief
metadata.description_blurb: Most Claude prompts fail not because the task is wrong but because five of the six anatomy slots are empty. Showing both prompts on one screen makes the gap undeniable.

beats.narration_text: Most Claude prompts fail not because the task is wrong, but because five of the six anatomy slots are empty. The task slot is filled. The other five are not. Fill them and the output improves. Leave them empty and Claude fills them with best guesses — which are often not your guesses. This is Liam, in for Bear.
beats.new_visual_element: ClaudeComposerAsk — cold open, vague summary prompt

props.command: Summarize this report for me. [20-page government report pasted here]

beats.narration_text: Slot one: the task. The test is whether the verb is testable. 'Help me' fails — it describes a relationship, not an operation. 'Summarize' passes. 'Extract the three key policy changes and flag any that contradict the executive summary' passes more. Testable verb means you can describe what a passing output looks like.
beats.new_visual_element: Task verb test — testable verb vs untestable

beats.narration_text: Slot two: context. Context is only the information that changes the output if absent. Audience, source document, the situation that makes this task different from the same task in a different situation. Everything else is noise — and noise costs tokens and pushes out what matters.
beats.new_visual_element: Context = delta only — audience, source, situation

beats.narration_text: Slot four: format. Five words, highest return on investment. 'Three sections, no jargon, four hundred words.' That sentence saves five minutes of editing and prevents the most common output defect — the essay format appearing when you wanted a list, or the list appearing when you needed prose.
beats.new_visual_element: Five-word format spec — most skipped, highest ROI

beats.narration_text: Slot six: evaluation criteria. The one slot everyone skips. Criteria turn review from interpretation into inspection. 'A non-specialist should be able to explain the main risk after reading this' — that is a criterion. 'Make it good' is not. Criteria precede the task description; writing them reveals requirements you did not consciously hold.

Five additions, two minutes of prompt writing, thirty-two minutes of review saved. Not because the six slots are magic — because they force you to articulate what you actually need before you delegate it. The human still reviews. The criteria just make the review faster and harder to skip.
beats.new_visual_element: Artifact view — six slots filled, before/after prompt comparison

beats.narration_text: Fill all six slots on your next prompt. Paste this — Claude will step you through each one before generating output.

props.command: Before generating anything, help me fill all six prompt slots: task (testable verb), context (delta only), constraints (prohibitions + positive rules), format (specific), examples (annotated), evaluation criteria (what would make this output wrong). Ask me for each one, then proceed.

beats.narration_text: If this saves thirty minutes on your next output, subscribe. Liam, in for Bear.
beats.new_visual_element: ClaudeTitleOutro — Six Slots, One Better Brief.
